feed.title: Linguistics Vanguard...
feed.subtitle: Latest articles from Linguistics Vanguard... (De Gruyter)

entry.title: The Ultraviolet Bleach corpus
entry.summary: Authors: Natalia Knoblock, Ryan Malkin DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lingvan-2020-0145 Pages: Page range: 229-241

entry.title: Is Zoom viable for sociophonetic research? A comparison of in-person and online recordings for vocalic analysis

entry.title: Translating COVID-19 information into Yiddish for the UK Hasidic community

entry.title: Is Zoom viable for sociophonetic research? A comparison of in-person and online recordings for sibilant analysis
entry.summary: Authors: Jeremy Calder, Rebecca Wheeler DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lingvan-2021-0014 Pages: Page range: 269-282

entry.title: Documentary linguists and risk communication: views from the virALLanguages project experience
entry.summary: Authors: Pierpaolo Di Carlo, Leonore Lukschy, Sydney Rey, Vasiliki Vita DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lingvan-2021-0021 Pages: Page range: 283-293

entry.title: Online data collection to address language sampling bias: lessons from the COVID-19 pandemic
entry.summary: Authors: Rowena Garcia, Jens Roeser, Evan Kidd DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lingvan-2021-0040 Pages: Page range: 295-306

entry.title: How did COVID-19 impact the use of Japanese complex words withmasuku‘mask’ in 2020?
entry.summary: Authors: Kiyoko Toratani DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lingvan-2021-0143 Pages: Page range: 307-323

entry.title: Semantic change and socio-semantic variation: the case of COVID-related neologisms on Reddit
entry.summary: Authors: Quirin Würschinger, Barbara McGillivray DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lingvan-2023-0106 Pages: Page range: 339-352

entry.title: On the role of metaphors in COVID-related political communication: an examination of Jacinda Ardern’s metaphorical language in managing the health crisis
entry.summary: Authors: Marta Degani DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/lingvan-2022-0101 Pages: Page range: 353-361
